text.title: onboarding-30-day Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for onboarding-30-day as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Including "review company values" or "explore documentation" without linking to a specific page or resource.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generic tasks are busywork that new hires complete by opening a random wiki page. Every task must point to a named resource; agents should fail loudly if the resource link is missing from the input context.

failure-mode.description: Applying US-style "celebrate Day 1" high-energy onboarding tone to DE/JP/UA new hires without localization.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Onboarding formality norms differ significantly across cultures. An overly casual, celebrations-heavy tone lands awkwardly in markets where professional distance is expected in the first weeks.

failure-mode.description: LLMs cargo-culting generic onboarding tasks without ingesting team-specific runbooks, producing generic output.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without actual workflow tools, team structure, and runbook context, agents auto-generate placeholder tasks that don't reflect the real onboarding experience. Require team-specific runbook input before generating a 30-day plan.

failure-mode.description: Calendar invites for week 1 generated without timezone awareness and conflict checking, producing overlapping events.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents auto-scheduling without read access to calendars create double-bookings and look unprofessional. Always validate freebusy before generating .ics invites.
